concerns.text: T009 (Train Classifier) is listed as parallel-safe [P] alongside T008 (Ablation Study), but T009 consumes the ground-truth utility labels generated by T008. T009 cannot execute until T008 completes. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' dependency rule.

T015 (Dynamic Layer Selection) depends on the trained classifier model. T014 (Proxy Validation) is a prerequisite gate for the validity of the training data (FR-006). While T014 doesn't produce the model, the plan implies the classifier is trained on ablation data *after* proxy validation. T015 should not be executable until the classifier is trained (which depends on T009), and T009's validity depends on T014 passing. The current ordering places T015 immediately after T014 without an explicit 'Train Classifier' task in US1, creating a missing link in the artifact flow.

T008 (Ablation Study) requires parsing raw logs to identify layers to remove and re-running the engine. T006 (Parser) extracts metrics from raw logs. T005 (Entropy) calculates metrics. The ablation study (T008) likely depends on the parser (T006) to understand the trajectory structure. While T008 is in Phase 2, it should strictly follow T006. The current list has them as parallel [P] or unordered, which is risky if T008 needs the parser's output format.

T014 (Proxy Validation) is listed under US1 Implementation. However, FR-006 requires a 'hold-out set' split. The task of splitting the data (creating the hold-out set) is missing from the task list. T014 assumes the split exists. This is a missing producer task for the data artifact required by T014.

T019 and T020 (Baseline Simulations) are in US2. T015 (Dynamic Simulation) is in US1. The aggregation task T021 depends on the output of T015, T019, and T020. The current ordering allows US2 to start before US1 is complete, which is fine for parallelism, but T021 (Aggregation) is in US2. If US2 is implemented before US1, T021 will fail because the Dynamic Policy results (from T015) do not exist yet. T021 must be ordered after T015 completes.
